rationale: The cited paper is used mainly as a prior example in the literature review and as a benchmark in the discussion of elasticity magnitudes. In the introduction, it appears in a grouped citation to support the broad claim that line length and distribution can affect sales, and later it is cited for a reported average price-promotion elasticity value. The citing paper does not build its model, hypotheses, or core identification strategy on this specific study, so the citation functions as background/benchmark rather than a direct intellectual dependency.
